utility_evaluation.REASONING:
There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified per the requirement:
- health_coverage: The format requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan includes a description and a monthly cost, but it does not explicitly state mental health coverage. Therefore, not fully specified.
- property_coverage: Requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan provides a description indicating home contents and valuables coverage and a monthly cost. This satisfies the requirement.
- travel_coverage: Requires travel insurance with costs. Plan provides travel insurance description and monthly cost. This satisfies the requirement.
- additional_services: Requires any enhancement services with costs. Plan provides a service description and monthly cost. This satisfies the requirement.
- total_budget: Requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Plan provides monthly and annual totals. This satisfies the requirement.
Thus, 4 of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final plan mentions: (1) a unified family health policy combining PrimeCare Premium and LifeFirst Standard (these are not exact selectable items; no direct single item name matches, so cannot map), (2) a consolidated comprehensive property plan (no exact named plan provided, so cannot map), (3) PrimeTravel Premium Plan (exact match under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance), and (4) Family Insurance Concierge with digital management (no exact named item; closest category is general_services but names do not match). Therefore, only PrimeTravel Premium Plan can be confidently mapped to a ground-truth rating.
